description: A policy with no outright false statements, but several mandatory GDPR disclosures are entirely absent - this file is designed to test Gap Hunter, not the Compliance Analyst.

gaps.missing_requirement: No data retention period or criteria is disclosed anywhere in the policy.
gaps.explanation: Article 13(2)(a) requires disclosure of the storage period, or the criteria used to determine it. This policy never mentions how long check-in entries, habit data, or account information is retained.

gaps.missing_requirement: No mention of the data subject's rights (access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection) anywhere in the policy.
gaps.explanation: Article 13(2)(b) requires informing data subjects of their rights. This policy contains zero mention of any GDPR right - no right to access, erase, or port data is described at all.

gaps.missing_requirement: No mention of the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
gaps.explanation: Article 13(2)(d) requires disclosing the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. Not mentioned anywhere.

gaps.missing_requirement: No identification of a Data Protection Officer or statement that none is required/appointed.
gaps.explanation: Article 13(1)(b) requires the contact details of the DPO, where applicable. The policy gives a generic contact email but never addresses DPO status at all (whether one exists or whether the company has assessed that one isn't required).

gaps.missing_requirement: No country/region specified for the third-party email and push-notification providers - no statement about whether any international transfer occurs.
gaps.explanation: Article 13(1)(f) requires disclosure of an intention to transfer data to a third country, including safeguards, if applicable. The policy mentions third-party processors but gives no indication of where they are located or whether a transfer outside the EEA occurs.
